title: Leak repair triage for scarce water
summary: Prioritizes leaks so stored water is not lost during outages or drought.
content: Leak repair triage for scarce water is a practical offline routine for leak control. Check storage containers, caps, taps, hoses, toilet tanks, roof tanks, and carrying containers on a schedule. Mark leaks as dripping, steady, spraying, structural, or unknown. Capture leaking water for non-drinking use if it has not touched contamination. Prioritize leaks threatening drinking water, medicine, baby care, or structural damage. Do only low-risk tightening or isolation; avoid pressurized, electrical, gas, or structural hazards. The goal is to make the action repeatable by a tired person, visible to helpers, and safe enough to stop when official help or a safer option appears.

warnings: Use Leak repair triage for scarce water as practical support, not as a replacement for official guidance, trained help, or evacuation when needed.

Filtering alone does not make chemically contaminated, fuel-contaminated, sewage-contaminated, or floodwater safe. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Leak repair triage for scarce water”.
